name: Air and Liquid Systems Corp. v. Devries
question: Can products liability defendants be held liable under maritime law for injuries caused by products that they did not make, sell, or distribute?
description: A case in which the Court held that under maritime law, a manufacturer has a duty to warn users of the dangers of its product if the product requires incorporation of a part produced by a third party, the resulting fully incorporated product is likely to be dangerous for its intended uses, and the manufacturer has no reason to believe that the product’s users would be aware of that danger.
